text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository (a React date-picker component library) at the commit where the gap below applies. Find and fix this in the library source.

Feature request: every rendered month in the calendar shows a navigation header (previous/next month arrows plus the current month/year label, with optional month/year dropdowns). Today that header always renders at the very top of the calendar, above the day grid, and there is no way to change that. Some consumers want the header to sit visually closer to the day grid it controls instead — for example in the middle of the calendar (between rows of days) or at the bottom (below the day grid) — but no prop exists to request that.

Add a new `monthHeaderPosition` prop, accepted by `DatePicker` (and threaded through to the underlying `Calendar`), with three possible string values: "top", "middle", and "bottom". It should default to "top", which preserves today's only behavior (header above the grid, shared navigation buttons at the top of the whole calendar).

When set to "middle" or "bottom", the navigation header for a month should be moved out of the single, calendar-wide top header area and attached to that month's own container instead — positioned within the day-grid area for "middle", or below the day grid for "bottom" — rather than living outside/above it. It must remain fully functional in either position: the month/year (and combined month-year, when in that mode) dropdowns still need to work, previous/next navigation still needs to work including correctly disabling near a configured `minDate`/`maxDate`, and a caller supplying a custom header via `renderCustomHeader` should still see their custom header rendered in the requested position.

When multiple months are shown at once (via the `monthsShown` prop) and `monthHeaderPosition` is "middle" or "bottom", each rendered month must get its own independent header in its own container — not one shared header for the whole calendar.

This will touch more than one component: the calendar-level component currently owns rendering of the single shared top header and the previous/next buttons, the per-month component will need the ability to optionally render a header block around its own day grid, and the prop needs to be threaded through from the public `DatePicker` API down through the popper/positioning wiring so everything stays consistent.

Environment notes: this is a Node/TypeScript project managed with Yarn (Berry) via Corepack; dependencies are already installed (re-run `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable` if you ever need to). Tests use Jest — run a single file with `node_modules/.bin/jest <path>`. Fix the library source under `src/`; do not just add workarounds in test files.

text: I’ll trace the existing header rendering and prop flow, then implement the positioning modes and run focused Jest/TypeScript checks. I’ll also do one targeted Nowledge Mem search for any prior datepicker/header decisions before changing behavior.

input.query: react-datepicker month header position top middle bottom custom header monthsShown

content.text: {"results":[{"id":"e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67","memory_id":"e4fae2ba-9738-42af-80e7-e9a191604a67","title":"react-datepicker multi-month custom header navigation reset","content":"In react-datepicker, day selection stores the visible panel index in DatePicker state.monthSelectedIn, which Calendar uses to offset the rendered months. A custom header's changeMonth callback changes Calendar's internal date but does not clear that index. The fix is to adapt renderCustomHeader in DatePicker and reset monthSelectedIn to 0 before delegating to the Calendar callback, preserving panel-origin behavior for day selection while making custom month jumps start at the leftmost panel. Regression coverage belongs in the DatePicker test suite with monthsShown={2}, a day click in the second month, and a custom-header changeMonth assertion.","relevance_score":0.8434999999999999,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (48%) + Text Match (30%) + Keyword Match (22%)"},{"id":"88046863-56eb-4f1f-8d0e-7de6a05ce5af","memory_id":"88046863-56eb-4f1f-8d0e-7de6a05ce5af","title":"Downshift aria-selected props are booleans","content":"Downshift useCombobox and useSelect getItemProps now return boolean aria-selected values directly from their comparisons. The prior template-literal coercion produced truthy string values; DOM aria-selected serialization remains unchanged. Focused regression assertions and the full Jest suite pass.","relevance_score":0.530465079307556,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (100%)"},{"id":"70599eb5-3c17-466e-84fe-1e457217a170","memory_id":"70599eb5-3c17-466e-84fe-1e457217a170","title":"Exclude adjacent-month filler cells from range preview","content":"In react-datepicker, Day.isInSelectingRange must first verify that the cell date's month matches the Month component's month prop. Calendar grids include adjacent-month filler cells, and date-range interval checks alone otherwise mark those cells with react-datepicker__day--in-selecting-range. The guard belongs in src/day.tsx so it also prevents selecting-range start/end modifiers from being applied to filler cells. Standalone Day tests must provide a realistic month prop (getMonth(day)).","relevance_score":0.509107091060929,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (78%) + Keyword Match (13%) + Text Match (9%)"},{"id":"eef8feaf-e321-49db-9719-37df017e8787","memory_id":"eef8feaf-e321-49db-9719-37df017e8787","title":"useSelect type-ahead Space and multi-character matching","content":"Downshift useSelect type-ahead behavior: a Space key extends the active inputValue query (so labels containing spaces can match), but standalone Space retains toggle/select semantics. Character matching skips the current highlight only for a one-character query; for longer queries it includes the current highlighted item, and starts at index 0 when no valid highlight exists.","relevance_score":0.46302390509843816,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (100%)"},{"id":"9a252eba-52f8-4c23-a51e-39142df3497d","memory_id":"9a252eba-52f8-4c23-a51e-39142df3497d","title":"useMultipleSelection deletion requires a valid activeIndex","content":"In downshift useMultipleSelection, SelectedItemKeyDownBackspace and SelectedItemKeyDownDelete must be no-ops when activeIndex is -1 or outside selectedItems. The reducer now guards activeIndex < 0 or >= selectedItems.length before slicing; regression tests cover no active item and stale index.","relevance_score":0.46296067202091207,"relevance_reason":"Semantic Match (100%)"}],"total":5,"query":"react-datepicker month header position top middle bottom custom header monthsShown","retrieval_mode":"strict","retrieval_space_ids":["default"]}

text: The current code already renders each month’s title/dropdowns inside its month container, while the arrow buttons are calendar-wide siblings. I’ll preserve that structure for `"top"`, and for `"middle"`/`"bottom"` pass the header into `Month`, where it can be placed around that month’s day grid; localized arrows will use each month’s displayed date for disabled-state calculation.

The implementation also keeps the public prop type constrained to the three requested literals and defaults it in both `Calendar` and `DatePicker`, while the existing popper path forwards it through the normal calendar prop spread.

input.title: react-datepicker monthHeaderPosition implementation
input.content: Implemented monthHeaderPosition as a public DatePicker/Calendar prop with top (default), middle, and bottom modes. Top preserves the existing month-container header and calendar-wide navigation; middle/bottom pass each month’s header into Month around/below its day grid, with localized previous/next buttons and month-date-based min/max disabled checks. TypeScript and focused Calendar/DatePicker Jest suites pass.
